stat: Estimated sterling saving per employee per year from applying McKinsey's reported 10-20% AI cost reduction (software engineering, IT and manufacturing functions) to the fully loaded cost of a median UK employee
method: Recipe A — sterling conversion applying a published cost-reduction percentage to ONS UK median employee cost data

sources_used.title: Employee earnings in the UK: 2025 (ASHE 2025 provisional results)
Rates and thresholds for employers 2026 to 2027
The state of AI: How organizations are rewiring to capture value

workings: ONS ASHE 2025: median gross annual earnings for full-time employees in post at least a year, April 2025 = GBP 39,039. Employer NI 2026/27: 15% on earnings above the GBP 5,000 secondary threshold = (39,039 - 5,000) x 0.15 = GBP 5,105.85. Employer minimum pension (3% on qualifying earnings band GBP 6,240-GBP 50,270, unchanged for 2026/27) = (39,039 - 6,240) x 0.03 = GBP 983.97. Fully loaded employer cost = 39,039 + 5,105.85 + 983.97 = GBP 45,128.82, rounded to GBP 45,100. Applying McKinsey's reported 10-20% cost reduction in software engineering, IT and manufacturing functions: low end 45,100 x 0.10 = GBP 4,510; high end 45,100 x 0.20 = GBP 9,020. Rounded to a GBP 4,500-GBP 9,000 per employee per year range.
